campaigns.title: SVX — Webinar Re-engagement
campaigns.webinarAngle: Exclusivity — SVX investor audience gets early access to the Climate Tech Exit Lab framework before it goes public. BTC brings the content, SVX brings the list.
campaigns.ecosystemNote: SVX is a CAFIID member. Both Keyona and Bryan have equal social capital on this contact.
campaigns.waitingOn: James's response. Sent by Keyona Apr 6 2026.
campaigns.emailSubject: Picking back up — webinar idea for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey James,

Hope the new year is treating you well. We connected late last year and I've been thinking about a collaboration that could be a good fit for your community.

We're putting together a webinar series on climate tech exits — breaking down how exits actually work in this space for small-check investors who want to understand the landscape before they commit capital. It's practical, framework-driven content that honestly doesn't exist anywhere else right now.

I'd love to co-host one session with SVX. You bring the audience, we bring the content. Low lift on your end, real value for your community.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if it makes sense?

Bryan

campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar collab — want to co-host one session?
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Bryan,

Hope you're feeling rested after the long weekend!

Bryan Duarte and I have been talking about doing more collaborative content this year, and he mentioned you and your work with both Clean Tech North and the Ontario Clean Tech Industry Association. We think there are some natural synergies there.

We're launching the Climate Tech Exit Lab this year — an initiative for both founders and investors on what goes into successful climate tech investments and exits. We'd love to co-host one session that we could bring to both communities.

Would you be open to a quick conversation to see if it's a fit?

Bryan

IC Onboarding Email — Investment Committee (all 4 on one thread)
campaigns.targetAudience: BTC Investment Committee — Melissa Allen, John Nicholson, Bryan Watson, Allison Gibson
campaigns.emailSubject: Investment Committee — first meeting, how it works, and what to expect
campaigns.emailDraft:
Melissa, John, Bryan, Allison,

Welcome to the Black Tech Capital Investment Committee, and thank you for signing on as Venture Partners. This email has everyone on it intentionally so you have each other's contact information from the start.

Here is what to expect over the next several weeks.

Scheduling. Bryan Duarte will be sending a scheduling poll shortly with options in the third and fourth week of May. Our first meeting will be two hours, held virtually.

Materials. You will receive everything you need to review by the end of April. That gives you a full two business weeks before the meeting. Please come having read the materials.

Questions. There are no stupid questions here. We want this to be a genuine exchange, and we are actively building a culture of curiosity at BTC. That said, before you send a question, please check whether it has already been answered in the materials provided. For any questions that remain, Bryan Duarte is your point of contact. All questions directed at founders under review will go through Bryan and me so that we can ensure nothing gets repeated and everything gets answered well.

Experts. If you encounter something in the materials where you would like an outside perspective before forming a view, we encourage you to look in your own network first. If you find someone, that is genuinely valuable to us, and we would love to know who it is so we can refer to them for future deal work. If you cannot find the right expertise, reach out to Bryan and we will find the right person.

Community. You are encouraged but not required to reach out to each other directly for introductions or one on ones ahead of the meeting. We think the relationships you build across this committee will be as valuable as the work itself.

More context is coming. We will be in touch soon with more information about our broader team and what is on the horizon for BTC. We are close to our minimum viable fund size and moving into an exciting phase.

Thank you again for being here. We are glad to have you.

Keyona
Black Tech Capital

campaigns.title: BTC Full Team Intro Email — All 8 Venture Partners
campaigns.targetAudience: All BTC Venture Partners — Advisory Board + Investment Committee
campaigns.emailSubject: Welcome to the BTC team — meet your fellow Venture Partners
campaigns.emailDraft:
Jade, Nicholas, Marlon, Lindsey, Melissa, John, Bryan, Allison,

I wanted to take a moment to introduce everyone formally. This email has both our Advisory Board and Investment Committee on it so you have each other's contact information and a sense of the full team we have built.

A quick note on titles. Your roles are listed as Advisory Board Member or Investment Committee Member, and both carry the Venture Partner designation as well. That is not just a label. You are all Venture Partners at Black Tech Capital, you are all receiving carry, and that is by design.

Advisory Board

Jade Lockard is a venture ecosystem builder with over five years advising 150+ founders, raising $50M+, and establishing the largest HBCU-focused founder network.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/jade-lockard

Nicholas Parker is a sustainable innovation visionary known as the father of cleantech, with over 40 years of global experience in the space.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/nicholasparker3

Marlon Thompson is the founder of Opening Round and Future Capital, which was acquired in 2023. He currently serves as a Venture Scout at Zeal Capital Partners.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/thompsonmarlon

Lindsey Motlow investigates emerging technologies in the energy industry with a focus on hydrogen, low carbon power generation, and advanced nuclear.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/lindseymotlow

Investment Committee

Melissa Allen is an award-winning emerging finance leader recognized by The Peak, Globe and Mail, and Bay Street Bull as a changemaker in her field.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/melissaaallen

John Nicholson brings over 30 years of experience in environmental engineering consulting, cleantech startups, and regulatory compliance.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/johnjnicholsonjr

Bryan Watson is a cleantech specialist with expertise in SR&ED tax credits, sustainable procurement, and eSports. He is a 2021 and 2024 Clean50 Awardee.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/cleantechnorth

Allison Gibson is an executive leader who mixes grassroots hustle with executive polish. She builds programs that uplift overlooked entrepreneurs and champions food ventures.
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/alliegco

A note on how Advisory Board and Investment Committee members relate to each other. Our Investment Committee members are encouraged to look to the Advisory Board as a resource. These are experienced operators and domain experts who are here and available for reference as you do your work. If a question or topic comes up during your review process where Advisory Board expertise would be useful, please do not hesitate to reach out to them directly.

What is coming.

We are very close to our minimum viable fund size and expect to get there before the end of the month. That is exciting, and it unlocks the next phase of what we are building.

Toward the end of Q2, we will be hosting a proper engagement meeting for the full team where we can connect, share deeper context, and talk about how each of you can mobilize on BTC's behalf as we grow.

Content features! One of the ways we build trust as a fund is by putting our team out front. We will be doing individual content features with each of you, spotlighting your work, your perspective, and your connection to the climate tech space. Look out for a message from us on that soon.

In the meantime, one on ones between any of you are encouraged. There is no requirement, just an open invitation.

Thank you for being here. More to come.

Keyona
Black Tech Capital

campaigns.title: LPAC Scope Email — Ebenezer Arthur / BKR Capital
campaigns.targetAudience: Ebenezer Arthur, BKR Capital — LPAC member representing BKR's LP position
campaigns.usageNote: Send to Ebenezer first. Wait for his approval or feedback before putting all LPAC members on a shared thread together.
campaigns.emailSubject: BTC Catalyst Fund — LPAC engagement overview
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hi Ebenezer,

Thank you again for your engagement with the BTC Catalyst Fund. Since the LPA includes provisions for an LP Advisory Committee, I wanted to reach out with a clear overview of how we are thinking about engaging the LPAC so we are aligned before we bring members together.

Here is our approach in plain language.

What the LPAC is. The LP Advisory Committee is a consultative body. Its role is to provide advice and opinions to the General Partner when requested, when required by the LPA, or when the committee chooses to offer input. It has no decision-making power over the fund's investments or operations. All investment and management decisions remain with the General Partner and Manager.

What LPAC members are responsible for. Members are expected to act in good faith in carrying out committee activities. They are not required to consider interests beyond those of the Limited Partner they represent, and they do not take on fiduciary duties to the fund or other partners. Their liability exposure is limited to good faith participation.

When the LPAC is engaged. The General Partner will bring matters to the LPAC when the LPA requires it, which includes: certain conflict of interest disclosures, annual reporting on management fees and carried interest, notification of any material legal proceedings involving the fund, and certain amendments that require committee approval. Outside of those required touchpoints, the General Partner may also seek the LPAC's input on an as-needed basis.

Membership. The LPA requires a minimum of three and a maximum of five members, each representing a Limited Partner with a commitment of at least $50,000. No LP may have more than one representative on the committee.

Next step. Once you have had a chance to review this and let me know if anything needs adjusting, we will move forward with getting all LPAC members on a shared email thread together so introductions can happen.

Please let me know if you have any questions or feedback.

Warmly,
Keyona
Black Tech Capital
